#+TITLE: IR → Excalidraw direct-render transform
#+STARTUP: showall

* What this fixture is

A hand-authored target =03-bank.excalidraw= against which the future
=drawl.emit.excalidraw= emitter will be diffed. Coordinates are produced
by the recursive containment layout described below — no external
layout tool (no dot, no viz.js). Pin which fields the emitter actually
touches before writing code.

* Layout algorithm (single pass over the IR)

The IR is already a containment tree. Render it as one.

** Constants

| name           | px  | notes                                       |
|----------------+-----+---------------------------------------------|
| =LEAF_H=         |  36 | every leaf shape's height                   |
| =LEAF_MIN_W=     |  72 | min leaf width                              |
| =CHAR_W=         |   9 | rough advance for fontSize=16, fontFamily=5 |
| =PAD_LABEL=      |  24 | horizontal padding inside a leaf shape      |
| =PAD_SIBLING=    |  24 | gap between sibling shapes                  |
| =PAD_INNER=      |  16 | inset on left/right/bottom of a frame       |
| =FRAME_NAME_H=   |  28 | inset on top of a frame for its name        |
| =LABEL_H=        |  20 | bound text element height                   |

** Recursive ~size~

#+begin_src
size(el):
  if el has no children:
    w = max(LEAF_MIN_W, length(label(el)) * CHAR_W + PAD_LABEL)
    h = LEAF_H
    return {w, h}
  else:
    children_sizes = [size(c) for c in el.children]
    inner_w = sum(c.w for c in children_sizes)
             + PAD_SIBLING * (count(children) - 1)
    inner_h = max(c.h for c in children_sizes)
    return {w: inner_w + 2 * PAD_INNER,
            h: inner_h + FRAME_NAME_H + PAD_INNER}
#+end_src

For v1 children flow left-to-right, single row. Wrap rule (~>4
children~: split to a 2-row grid) is a knob the emitter can carry,
not exercised in this fixture.

** Recursive ~place~

#+begin_src
place(el, origin_x, origin_y, frame_id):
  el_w, el_h = size(el)
  el.{x, y, w, h} = {origin_x, origin_y, el_w, el_h}
  el.frame_id    = frame_id

  if el has children:
    cursor_x = origin_x + PAD_INNER
    cursor_y = origin_y + FRAME_NAME_H
    for c in el.children:
      place(c, cursor_x, cursor_y, frame_id = el.id)
      cursor_x += size(c).w + PAD_SIBLING
#+end_src

Diagram top level:

#+begin_src
cursor_x = 0
for el in ir.elements:
  el_h = size(el).h
  y = (max_sibling_h - el_h) / 2          ; vertical centering on the tallest top-level sibling
  place(el, cursor_x, y, frame_id = null)
  cursor_x += size(el).w + PAD_SIBLING
#+end_src

** Worked numbers for =03-bank-containers.drawl=

Diagram elements: =customer= (leaf), =bank= (frame w/ 4 children), =mainframe= (leaf).

| element   | label              | chars | w calc           |    w |  h |
|-----------+--------------------+-------+------------------+------+----|
| customer  | Banking Customer   |    16 | 16·9+24          |  168 | 36 |
| webapp    | Web App            |     7 | 7·9+24           |   87 | 36 |
| spa       | SPA                |     3 | max(72, 3·9+24)  |   72 | 36 |
| api       | API                |     3 | max(72, 3·9+24)  |   72 | 36 |
| db        | Database           |     8 | 8·9+24           |   96 | 36 |
| mainframe | Mainframe Banking  |    17 | 17·9+24          |  177 | 36 |

Bank frame inner row width: =87 + 72 + 72 + 96 + 3*24 = 399=. Outer: =399 + 2*16 = 431=
wide, =36 + 28 + 16 = 80= tall.

Top-level siblings (left→right, vertical-center on tallest = bank, =h=80=):

| el        |   x |  y |   w |  h |
|-----------+-----+----+-----+----|
| customer  |   0 | 22 | 168 | 36 |
| bank      | 192 |  0 | 431 | 80 |
| mainframe | 647 | 22 | 177 | 36 |

Bank's children, inner cursor starts at =(192+16, 0+28) = (208, 28)=:

| el     |   x |  y |  w |  h |
|--------+-----+----+----+----|
| webapp | 208 | 28 | 87 | 36 |
| spa    | 319 | 28 | 72 | 36 |
| api    | 415 | 28 | 72 | 36 |
| db     | 511 | 28 | 96 | 36 |

These are the numbers the fixture must produce. The fixture is the
contract.

* Element mapping (drawl → excalidraw =type=)

| drawl                   | excalidraw =type=     | extra                  |
|-------------------------+---------------------+------------------------|
| =:person=                 | =ellipse=             |                        |
| =:system= leaf            | =rectangle=           | ~roundness: {type: 3}~   |
| =:system= with children   | (skipped — see below) |                        |
| =:container=              | =rectangle=           | ~roundness: {type: 3}~   |
| =:component=              | =rectangle=           |                        |
| =:role :database=         | =ellipse=             | cylinder approximation |
| =:role :queue=            | =rectangle=           | ~strokeStyle: "dashed"~  |
| =:external= attr          | (any of the above)  | ~strokeStyle: "dashed"~  |

A =:system= with children emits a single =frame= element (with =name= =
the system title) and no surrounding rectangle. Each child carries
~frameId~ pointing at the frame's id. The frame's bbox comes from
~size()~ above. =:container= with children works the same way.

* Edge transform

For each edge with ~from~, ~to~, label:

1. Look up the placed from/to shapes (~{x, y, w, h}~).
2. Pick anchor points along the shape edges:
   - same row (=|from.y_mid - to.y_mid| < LEAF_H/2=):
     start = ~(from.x + from.w, from.y_mid)~,
     end   = ~(to.x,            to.y_mid)~
   - else: center→center; Excalidraw clips via binding gap.
3. Emit:

#+begin_src js
{ type: "arrow",
  x: start_x, y: start_y,
  width:  end_x - start_x,
  height: end_y - start_y,
  points: [[0, 0], [end_x - start_x, end_y - start_y]],
  startBinding: { elementId: from_id, focus: 0, gap: 4 },
  endBinding:   { elementId: to_id,   focus: 0, gap: 4 },
  startArrowhead: null,           // "arrow" for bidirectional
  endArrowhead:   "arrow" }
#+end_src

Critical: the arrow's id MUST also appear in each endpoint shape's
~boundElements~ as ={id, type: "arrow"}~. One-sided binding visually
attaches but detaches on drag.

For v1 every edge is a straight segment. Curved/elbowed routing is a
later knob if needed.

* Labels (text-on-shape, text-on-arrow)

For every shape and arrow, emit a separate ~text~ element with:

- ~containerId~ = the host shape/arrow id
- ~originalText~ = the user label (Excalidraw will rewrap into ~text~)
- ~text~       = same as originalText for first emit; loader recomputes
- ~fontSize: 16~ (Excalidraw =NORMAL_FONT_SIZE=)
- ~fontFamily: 5~ (Excalifont, current default; numeric, NOT a string)
- ~textAlign: "center"~, ~verticalAlign: "middle"~, ~lineHeight: 1.25~

For a shape-bound label: ~width~ = container width, ~height~ = =LABEL_H=,
~y~ = container ~y + (h - LABEL_H)/2~.

For an arrow-bound label: place above the arrow midpoint
(~y = arrow_y - LABEL_H - 2~).

The host's ~boundElements~ MUST include ={id: textId, type: "text"}~.
For a shape with both a label and arrows attached, ~boundElements~ holds
all of them in one array.

* Identity

Deterministic, diffable:

| element     | id                              |
|-------------+---------------------------------|
| shape       | =drawl:<source-id>=               |
| shape label | =drawl:<source-id>:label=         |
| frame       | =drawl:<source-id>=               |
| edge        | =drawl:e:<from-id>:<to-id>=       |
| edge label  | =drawl:e:<from-id>:<to-id>:label= |

~seed~, ~versionNonce~ = =(hash id)= as a 32-bit int.
~version: 1~, ~updated: 0~ for fully reproducible output.

This fixture uses small hand-picked integers (1001..2041) so the diff
against a real emitter run only fails on layout math, not on PRNG
values.

* Envelope

Required to open cleanly in excalidraw.com:

#+begin_src js
{ type: "excalidraw",
  version: 2,
  source: "drawl",
  appState: { viewBackgroundColor: "#ffffff",
              gridSize: null,
              gridStep: 5,
              theme: "light" },
  files: {},
  elements: [...] }
#+end_src

=version: 2= is the file-format version (unrelated to per-element ~version~).

* Gotchas to keep in mind when writing the emitter

1. ~boundElements~ on a shape is one array carrying ALL bound things —
   the shape's label AND any incident arrows AND any other bound text.
   It is not separated by type.
2. Arrow ~points~ are local to the arrow's ~x,y~. First point must be
   ~[0,0]~. Forget either and the arrow renders in the wrong place.
3. ~fontFamily~ is a number (5 = Excalifont). Passing the string
   "Excalifont" silently breaks text rendering.
4. ~frameId~ goes on each child; the frame does NOT carry a child list.
   A child can only belong to one frame.
5. ~roundness~: shapes use ~{type: 3}~ for proportional rounded corners;
   arrows use ~{type: 2}~ for curved. =null= for ellipse / text / frame.
6. Bidirectional bindings: setting only ~startBinding~/~endBinding~ on
   the arrow without mirroring it in shape ~boundElements~ produces an
   arrow that *looks* attached but detaches the moment the user drags
   the shape. Both sides, always.
7. =version: 2= in the envelope is the file format version — unrelated
   to per-element ~version~.

* What this fixture unblocks

- A pure ~drawl.emit.excalidraw/emit [ir] → string~ — no IO, no shell.
- Same emitter runs identically on JVM (clojure/babashka) and CLJS
  (browser SPA). The SPA can offer a "Download .excalidraw" button
  with zero extra runtime work.
- A Babashka CLI ~bb drawl compile --backend excalidraw~ that's a thin
  wrapper around ~drawl.compiler/compile~.
- Snapshot tests that diff a future emitter run against this fixture
  catch any layout-constant drift.
